speaker: RICHMOND
text: Fellows in arms, and my most loving friends,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Bruised underneath the yoke of tyranny,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Thus far into the bowels of the land

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Have we marched on without impediment,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: And here receive we from our father Stanley

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Lines of fair comfort and encouragement.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The wretched, bloody, and usurping boar,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: That spoiled your summer fields and fruitful vines,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Swills your warm blood like wash, and makes his trough

speaker: RICHMOND
text: In your embowelled bosoms—this foul swine

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Is now even in the center of this isle,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Near to the town of Leicester, as we learn.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: From Tamworth thither is but one day’s march.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: In God’s name, cheerly on, courageous friends,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: To reap the harvest of perpetual peace

speaker: RICHMOND
text: By this one bloody trial of sharp war.

speaker: OXFORD
text: Every man’s conscience is a thousand men

speaker: OXFORD
text: To fight against this guilty homicide.

speaker: HERBERT
text: I doubt not but his friends will turn to us.

speaker: BLUNT
text: He hath no friends but what are friends for fear,

speaker: BLUNT
text: Which in his dearest need will fly from him.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: All for our vantage. Then, in God’s name, march.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: True hope is swift, and flies with swallow’s wings;

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Kings it makes gods, and meaner creatures kings.
